issues: Single monolithic JS bundle (513KB) exceeds 500KB Vite warning threshold
No React.lazy or dynamic imports — everything loaded upfront
No React.memo on list-rendered components (TaskRow, DaySection, StatsCards)
No useMemo/useCallback for expensive computations or handlers in App.tsx
No manual chunks in vite.config.ts — vendor code mixed with app code

plan: Add React.lazy for AIAssistant, TeamPanel, RemindersPanel, TaskEditModal, FilePreviewModal
Add Suspense boundaries with Skeleton fallbacks
Configure vite manualChunks: react-vendor, dnd-kit, insforge-sdk, lucide-icons
Add React.memo to TaskRow, DaySection, StatsCards, StatsPills
Add useCallback for all event handlers passed as props in App.tsx
Add useMemo for filtered task data

audit.summary: React+Vite weekly planner app with 46 TS/TSX files, 4373 LOC. Single 513KB JS bundle exceeds Vite warning. No code splitting, no lazy loading, no React.memo, no error boundaries, no TypeScript strict mode, no theme system.

issues: tsconfig.json has NO strict:true — implicit any, unchecked nulls everywhere
No noUncheckedIndexedAccess — array/object access is unsafe
No error boundaries — any component crash takes down entire app
auth parameter typed as { user: any; signOut: () => Promise<void> } — any leaks
useWeeklyPlan hook has no explicit return type (1000+ line file)
useOpenClaw hook has no explicit return type
No React.StrictMode in main.tsx entry point
plan: Enable strict:true and noUncheckedIndexedAccess in tsconfig.json
Fix all resulting TypeScript errors
Create ErrorBoundary component in src/components/ui/
Wrap main sections and lazy-loaded components with ErrorBoundary
Add proper types replacing any usage
Add return types to exported hook functions

issues: No theme system — hardcoded dark theme only, no light mode option
No theme toggle in header
No micro-interactions on task cards (no hover scale, no press feedback)
No smooth expand/collapse animation for day sections (instant toggle)
EmptyState component exists but may not cover all empty scenarios
Skeleton component exists but not used as Suspense fallbacks
plan: Create useTheme hook with ThemeProvider (dark/light/system)
Add CSS variables for theme colors, use Tailwind dark: variants
Add theme toggle button in header with sun/moon icons
Add hover:scale-[1.01] on task cards, active:scale-[0.98] on buttons
Add smooth height animation for day section expand/collapse
Use Skeleton as Suspense fallback for lazy components

instructions: - Fix all resulting TypeScript errors across the codebase
- Add proper return types to exported functions
- Replace any `any` types with proper types where feasible
- Create src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx - a reusable error boundary component
- It should show a friendly error UI with a "Try Again" button
- Style it consistently with the existing design system (dark theme, rounded-xl, etc.)
- Wrap main App content sections with ErrorBoundary
- Wrap each lazy-loaded component Suspense with ErrorBoundary
- Add explicit return types to hook functions
4. Make sure the build passes after all changes
Return JSON: { summary: string, changes: string[], strictErrors: number, errorsFixed: number }
prompt.role: TypeScript engineer focused on code quality
